initial_program:
% ----------  basic counts (optional) ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, Color)}.

% ----------  global maximum colour ----------
globalMax(M) :- M = #max { Color : input(_, _, Color) }.

% ----------  max colour in a particular row ----------
max_in_row(R, MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { Color : input(R, C, Color) }.

% max in the previous row (R1)
max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev) :- MaxPrev = #max { Color : input(R-1, C, Color) }.

% max in the next row (R+1)
max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext) :- MaxNext = #max { Color : input(R+1, C, Color) }.

% index of the last row (0based)
lastRow := #max { R : input(R, _, _) }.

% ----- block of rows with a rowmax smaller than the global maximum -----
% blockStart  first row of a block
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, (R = 0).
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev), MaxPrev != MaxR.

% blockEnd  last row of a block
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, (R = lastRow).
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext), MaxNext != MaxR.

% first row of a block (unless the block reaches the bottom)
zeroRow(R) :- blockStart(R), not blockEnd(R).

% last row of a block that reaches the bottom of the grid
zeroEndRow(R) :- blockEnd(R), R = lastRow.

% ----- decide colour of each output cell -----
output_cell(R, C) :-
    (zeroRow(R) or zeroEndRow(R)), Color = 0.
output_cell(R, C) :-
    not (zeroRow(R) or zeroEndRow(R)), Color = input(R, C, Color).

% ----- final output cells -----
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

rounds.program_after:
% ----------  basic counts (optional) ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, Color)}.

% ----------  global maximum colour ----------
globalMax(M) :- M = #max { Color : input(_, _, Color) }.

% ----------  max colour in a particular row ----------
max_in_row(R, MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { Color : input(R, C, Color) }.

% max in the previous row (R1)
max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev) :- MaxPrev = #max { Color : input(R-1, C, Color) }.

% max in the next row (R+1)
max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext) :- MaxNext = #max { Color : input(R+1, C, Color) }.

% index of the last row (0based)
lastRow := #max { R : input(R, _, _) }.

% ----- block of rows with a rowmax smaller than the global maximum -----
% blockStart  first row of a block
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, (R = 0).
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev), MaxPrev != MaxR.

% blockEnd  last row of a block
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, (R = lastRow).
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext), MaxNext != MaxR.

% first row of a block (unless the block reaches the bottom)
zeroRow(R) :- blockStart(R), not blockEnd(R).

% last row of a block that reaches the bottom of the grid
zeroEndRow(R) :- blockEnd(R), R = lastRow.

% ----- decide colour of each output cell -----
output_cell(R, C) :-
    (zeroRow(R) or zeroEndRow(R)), Color = 0.
output_cell(R, C) :-
    not (zeroRow(R) or zeroEndRow(R)), Color = input(R, C, Color).

% ----- final output cells -----
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

rounds.program_after:
% ----------  basic counts (optional) ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, Color)}.

% ----------  global maximum colour ----------
globalMax(M) :- M = #max { Color : input(_, _, Color) }.

% ----------  max colour in a particular row ----------
max_in_row(R, MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { Color : input(R, C, Color) }.

% max in the previous row (R1)
max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev) :- MaxPrev = #max { Color : input(R-1, C, Color) }.

% max in the next row (R+1)
max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext) :- MaxNext = #max { Color : input(R+1, C, Color) }.

% index of the last row (0based)
lastRow :- #max { R : input(R, _, _) }.

% ----- block of rows with a rowmax smaller than the global maximum -----
% blockStart  first row of a block
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, R = 0.
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev), MaxPrev != MaxR.

% blockEnd  last row of a block
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, R = lastRow.
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext), MaxNext != MaxR.

% first row of a block (unless the block reaches the bottom)
zeroRow(R) :- blockStart(R), not blockEnd(R).

% last row of a block that reaches the bottom of the grid
zeroEndRow(R) :- blockEnd(R), R = lastRow.

% ----- decide colour of each output cell -----
output_cell(R, C) :-
zeroRow(R) or zeroEndRow(R), Color = 0.
output_cell(R, C) :-
not zeroRow(R), not zeroEndRow(R), Color = input(R, C, Color).

% ----- final output cells -----
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

rounds.program_after:
% ----------  basic counts (optional) ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, Color)}.

% ----------  global maximum colour ----------
globalMax(M) :- M = #max { Color : input(_, _, Color) }.

% ----------  max colour in a particular row ----------
max_in_row(R, MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { Color : input(R, C, Color) }.

% max in the previous row (R1)
max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev) :- MaxPrev = #max { Color : input(R-1, C, Color) }.

% max in the next row (R+1)
max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext) :- MaxNext = #max { Color : input(R+1, C, Color) }.

% index of the last row (0based)
lastRow :- #max { R : input(R, _, _) }.

% ----- block of rows with a rowmax smaller than the global maximum -----
% blockStart  first row of a block
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, R = 0.
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev), MaxPrev != MaxR.

% blockEnd  last row of a block
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, R = lastRow.
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext), MaxNext != MaxR.

% first row of a block (unless the block reaches the bottom)
zeroRow(R) :- blockStart(R), not blockEnd(R).

% last row of a block that reaches the bottom of the grid
zeroEndRow(R) :- blockEnd(R), R = lastRow.

% ----- decide colour of each output cell -----
output_cell(R, C) :-
zeroRow(R) or zeroEndRow(R), Color = 0.
output_cell(R, C) :-
not zeroRow(R), not zeroEndRow(R), Color = input(R, C, Color).

% ----- final output cells -----
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

final_program:
% ----------  basic counts (optional) ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, Color)}.

% ----------  global maximum colour ----------
globalMax(M) :- M = #max { Color : input(_, _, Color) }.

% ----------  max colour in a particular row ----------
max_in_row(R, MaxR) :- MaxR = #max { Color : input(R, C, Color) }.

% max in the previous row (R1)
max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev) :- MaxPrev = #max { Color : input(R-1, C, Color) }.

% max in the next row (R+1)
max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext) :- MaxNext = #max { Color : input(R+1, C, Color) }.

% index of the last row (0based)
lastRow :- #max { R : input(R, _, _) }.

% ----- block of rows with a rowmax smaller than the global maximum -----
% blockStart  first row of a block
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, R = 0.
blockStart(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_prev(R, MaxPrev), MaxPrev != MaxR.

% blockEnd  last row of a block
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax, R = lastRow.
blockEnd(R) :- max_in_row(R, MaxR), MaxR < globalMax,
               max_in_row_next(R, MaxNext), MaxNext != MaxR.

% first row of a block (unless the block reaches the bottom)
zeroRow(R) :- blockStart(R), not blockEnd(R).

% last row of a block that reaches the bottom of the grid
zeroEndRow(R) :- blockEnd(R), R = lastRow.

% ----- decide colour of each output cell -----
output_cell(R, C) :-
zeroRow(R) or zeroEndRow(R), Color = 0.
output_cell(R, C) :-
not zeroRow(R), not zeroEndRow(R), Color = input(R, C, Color).

% ----- final output cells -----
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
